1	-- $Id: testes/constructs.lua $
2	-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua
3	
4	;;print "testing syntax";;
5	
6	local debug = require "debug"
7	
8	
9	local function checkload (s, msg)
10	  assert(string.find(select(2, load(s)), msg))
11	end
12	
13	-- testing semicollons
14	local a
15	do ;;; end
16	; do ; a = 3; assert(a == 3) end;
17	;
18	
19	
20	-- invalid operations should not raise errors when not executed
21	if false then a = 3 // 0; a = 0 % 0 end
22	
23	
24	-- testing priorities
25	
26	assert(2^3^2 == 2^(3^2));
27	assert(2^3*4 == (2^3)*4);
28	assert(2.0^-2 == 1/4 and -2^- -2 == - - -4);
29	assert(not nil and 2 and not(2>3 or 3<2));
30	assert(-3-1-5 == 0+0-9);
31	assert(-2^2 == -4 and (-2)^2 == 4 and 2*2-3-1 == 0);
32	assert(-3%5 == 2 and -3+5 == 2)
33	assert(2*1+3/3 == 3 and 1+2 .. 3*1 == "33");
34	assert(not(2+1 > 3*1) and "a".."b" > "a");
35	
36	assert(0xF0 | 0xCC ~ 0xAA & 0xFD == 0xF4)
37	assert(0xFD & 0xAA ~ 0xCC | 0xF0 == 0xF4)
38	assert(0xF0 & 0x0F + 1 == 0x10)
39	
40	assert(3^4//2^3//5 == 2)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV

content.content:
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

file.content:
-- do not do too many combinations for soft tests
local level = _soft and 3 or 4

cases[1] = basiccases
for i = 2, level do cases[i] = createcases(i) end
print("+")

local i = 0
for n = 1, level do
  for _, v in pairs(cases[n]) do
    local s = v[1]
    local p = load(string.format(prog, s, s), "")
    IX = false
    assert(p() == v[2] and IX == not not v[2])
    i = i + 1
    if i % 60000 == 0 then print('+') end
  end
end
IX = nil
_G.GLOB1 = nil

-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end

-- $Id: testes/constructs.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

;;print "testing syntax";;

local debug = require "debug"


local function checkload (s, msg)
  assert(string.find(select(2, load(s)), msg))
end

-- testing semicollons
local a
do ;;; end
; do ; a = 3; assert(a == 3) end;
;


-- invalid operations should not raise errors when not executed
if false then a = 3 // 0; a = 0 % 0 end

content.content:
1560	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1561	// Protected parser
1562	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1563	
1564	/// Parser invocation data passed through `pcall`.
1565	///
1566	/// C: `struct SParser { ZIO *z; Mbuffer buff; Dyndata dyd; const char *mode; const char *name; }`
1567	///
1568	/// PORT NOTE: `const char *mode` and `const char *name` become owned byte vecs
1569	/// so that `SParser` can outlive the original string data without raw pointers.
1570	struct SParser {
1571	    z: ZIO,
1572	    /// LexBuffer from `crate::zio` (Mbuffer in C).
1573	    buff: LexBuffer,
1574	    /// TODO(phase-b): real Dyndata lives in the lua-parse crate.
1575	    dyd: DynDataStub,
1576	    // C: const char *mode — byte slice for chunk mode ("b", "t", or "bt")
1577	    // PORT NOTE: stored as Option<Vec<u8>> to own the bytes; None means no mode restriction.
1578	    mode: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1579	    // C: const char *name — chunk name (source identifier)
1580	    name: Vec<u8>,
1581	}
1582	
1583	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1584	///
1585	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1586	fn check_mode(
1587	    state: &mut LuaState,
1588	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1589	    kind: &[u8],
1590	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1591	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1592	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1593	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1594	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1595	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1596	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1597	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1598	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1599	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1600	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1601	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1602	            )));
1603	        }
1604	    }
1605	    Ok(())
1606	}
1607	
1608	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1609	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1610	///
1611	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1612	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1613	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1614	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1615	    let c = p.z.getc();
1616	
1617	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1618	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1619	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1620	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1621	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1622	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1623	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1624	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1625	    } else {
1626	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1627	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1628	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1629	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1630	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1631	    };
1632	
1633	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1634	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1635	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1636	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1637	
1638	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1639	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:

delta.text: 767` uses `opt_arg_string_bytes(3)` which returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (empty bytes) for missing args, never fal
ling through to the `b"bt"` default. So `load(s)` with no mode argument gets `mode=
""`, which fails the text-chunk mode check.

content.text: Found the root cause. `load_fn` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/base.rs:763-767` uses `opt_arg_string_bytes(3)` which returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (empty bytes) for missing args, never falling through to the `b"bt"` default. So `load(s)` with no mode argument gets `mode=""`, which fails the text-chunk mode check.

## Report

**Root cause:** `
crates/lua-stdlib/src/base.rs:763-767` (`load_fn`). The code attempted to default `mode` to `"bt"` via `opt_arg_string
bytes) — never `Err` — when arg 3 is missing/nil. So `load(s)` with no mode arg got `mode=""`, which then fail
ed `check_mode` in `do_.rs:1586` with `"attempt to load a text chunk (mode is '')"`. The failing test
path: `checkload("for x do", "expected")` → `load("for x do")` returns `(nil, "attempt to load a text chunk...

")` → `string.find(msg, "expected")` returns nil (no match) → `assert(nil)` fires "assertion failed!".

**Fix:** Repl
aced the broken call with `state.opt_arg_string(3, b"bt")?`, which is the proper helper that takes a default and returns it for None

/Nil args (already implemented at `state_stub.rs:435`).

**State after fix:** Verified man
ually that `load("for x do")` and `load("x:call")` now produce parser errors contain
ing "expected" (e.g. `'=' or 'in' expected`). I did not re-run the full constructs.lua per inst
ructions — the mega-loop will re-test. There is also a separate, smaller line-info off
-by-one issue (the assert-failed location pointed at the function's def line instead of the assert call line), but it's not the c
ause of this failure and out of scope.

content.text:
Build is green (only warnings, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/base.rs:763-767` (`load_fn`). The code attempted to default `mode` to `"bt"` via `opt_arg_string_bytes(3).unwrap_or_else(|_| b"bt".to_vec())`, but `opt_arg_string_bytes` returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (empty bytes) — never `Err` — when arg 3 is missing/nil. So `load(s)` with no mode arg got `mode=""`, which then failed `check_mode` in `do_.rs:1586` with `"attempt to load a text chunk (mode is '')"`. The failing test path: `checkload("for x do", "expected")` → `load("for x do")` returns `(nil, "attempt to load a text chunk...")` → `string.find(msg, "expected")` returns nil (no match) → `assert(nil)` fires "assertion failed!".

**Fix:** Replaced the broken call with `state.opt_arg_string(3, b"bt")?`, which is the proper helper that takes a default and returns it for None/Nil args (already implemented at `state_stub.rs:435`).

**State after fix:** Verified manually that `load("for x do")` and `load("x:call")` now produce parser errors containing "expected" (e.g. `'=' or 'in' expected`). I did not re-run the full constructs.lua per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. There is also a separate, smaller line-info off-by-one issue (the assert-failed location pointed at the function's def line instead of the assert call line), but it's not the cause of this failure and out of scope.

Build is green (only warnings, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/base.rs:763-767` (`load_fn`). The code attempted to default `mode` to `"bt"` via `opt_arg_string_bytes(3).unwrap_or_else(|_| b"bt".to_vec())`, but `opt_arg_string_bytes` returns `Ok(Vec::new())` (empty bytes) — never `Err` — when arg 3 is missing/nil. So `load(s)` with no mode arg got `mode=""`, which then failed `check_mode` in `do_.rs:1586` with `"attempt to load a text chunk (mode is '')"`. The failing test path: `checkload("for x do", "expected")` → `load("for x do")` returns `(nil, "attempt to load a text chunk...")` → `string.find(msg, "expected")` returns nil (no match) → `assert(nil)` fires "assertion failed!".

**Fix:** Replaced the broken call with `state.opt_arg_string(3, b"bt")?`, which is the proper helper that takes a default and returns it for None/Nil args (already implemented at `state_stub.rs:435`).

**State after fix:** Verified manually that `load("for x do")` and `load("x:call")` now produce parser errors containing "expected" (e.g. `'=' or 'in' expected`). I did not re-run the full constructs.lua per instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. There is also a separate, smaller line-info off-by-one issue (the assert-failed location pointed at the function's def line instead of the assert call line), but it's not the cause of this failure and out of scope.
